name: interp_log_length_equals_gtail
reason: `interp_log` uses `Seq::new(global_tail, ...)` so its length is always `gtail` — but `i_log` could contain entries beyond `gtail` that are silently dropped by the interpretation

name: refinement_at_zero
reason: Refinement at version 0 is trivially `init_spec() == init_spec()` — tests the vacuous base case

name: interp_log_ignores_extra_entries
reason: Entries at or beyond `gtail` are invisible to `interp_log` — the map-to-seq interpretation silently truncates, so appending entries doesn't change the interpreted log

name: refinement_proof_external_body
reason: `RefinementProof` is `external_body` with only a `PhantomData` field — its existence is unconditionally provable, meaning the refinement witness carries no actual proof content

name: state_at_version_node_id_irrelevant
reason: `node_id` is stored in `LogEntry` but completely ignored by `compute_nrstate_at_version` — `interp_log` strips it via `.op`, so the refinement erases node provenance entirely
